strengths: Deep expertise in high-throughput, event-driven microservices, demonstrated by architecting low-latency systems handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby.
Direct, hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure, including Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and agentic workflows at re:cinq.
Relevant domain exposure to fintech and high-stakes financial logic through building investment platforms at RIDE Capital and optimizing complex pricing strategies at Optilyz.
concerns: Lacks an explicit track record of executive-level (CTO) leadership or scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people in a high-growth environment.
Production experience with multi-agent AI systems appears relatively recent, which may not fully satisfy the requirement for extensive, multi-year AI production operation.
Missing direct experience with the specific financial domains of algorithmic working-capital allocation and real-time credit-risk modeling.
response.justification: Bogdan is a compelling technical candidate who bridges the gap between high-performance distributed systems and emerging AI-agent paradigms. His current work with agentic workflows and MCP generators at re:cinq directly aligns with the core mandate to architect tool-augmented, multi-agent frameworks. Furthermore, his proven ability to engineer ultra-low-latency, event-driven microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby provides the exact architectural foundation needed for real-time trade decisioning and inference. Combined with his domain exposure at RIDE Capital and a strong dedication to engineering rigor—evidenced by his open-source leadership and TDD advocacy—Bogdan possesses the foundational technical depth to architect a reliable, AI-native financial infrastructure.
